entry.title: Re: SAP ALV GRID Export to Excel issue -> After upgrade
entry.content: no

entry.title: Mass upload of Service Entry Sheet (SeS) for Lean services via Google sheets
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Is there a standard function in S4 HANA Private Cloud edition to have mass upload of Service entry sheets (SeS) for Lean services from a google sheet and create SeS in S4. 
I read that there an import functionality in Public Cloud, but could not find anything for Private Cloud. 

Thank you for your response

entry.title: Re: Understanding Joule - (Step #4) Joule Interaction Patterns
entry.content:
Hi pasupularajesh

Thanks. The diagram was created manually 😊

entry.title: Re: PR to PO quantity conversion issue
entry.content:
Hi,

         I found out the solution, In unit of measure (Order Unit) FT we have to maintain Decimal rounding and decimal places. Then in PO it will reflect 3 decimal places.

entry.title: MBLB Add New Fields
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I want to add new fields in MBLB screen. How can I do this?

entry.title: Re: IDOC mapping GSVERF02 --> GSVERF01
entry.content:
Usually the versions with higher number only have additional fields at the end of a segment or new segments.
The additional fields will be ignored in the downwards versions. The additional segments you can filter out easily by BD56 - see video : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7jgGp6LlvpA

But i have no idea how you can manipulate EDIDC-Record w/o coding. I assume you need to write a 3-lines user exit.

If you have a middleware (e.g. SAP PI, Integration Suite, or from other vendors) I would advice to use it.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: Connect to Sybsase DB using isql
entry.content: Thanks Bret!

entry.title: Re: Connect to Sybsase DB using isql
entry.content:
Thanks @Bret_Halford 

I changed to lower case and was able to connect with sapsa and sapsso users. 

sa didn't work.

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting- Data Migration & Cutover Approach
entry.content:
It depends on when you started with accounting  and what data you want to have in GR analytics.
You need to have a starting point in GR for the entire BS including the opening balances. If accounting activation was quite recent this could be achieved by your option 2 provided you take over all (!) available periods from accounting. Note you will need to run consolidation for all periods. Advantage is that you have all data in GR in all periods available for analytics eg comparison prior year etc.

Lower effort can be expected with option 1  also considering that meanwhile data release offers a YTD mode if you run it from the „schedule tasks for consolidation“ app.

The options may also depend on when you activated GRPL, group reporting preparation ledgers. Precondition for using the data release task is that GR fields are filled in accounting. Either from the start of accounting or after activation of GRPL in accounting balance carry forward.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Syntax error e28063, e28051, e28037 for the Procedure...
entry.content: make sure you choose the right Dependency type, these error messages look like you chose 'Precondition', 'Selection condition', right one should be 'Procedure'

entry.title: Re: Duplication AND connection in SAP dependencies
entry.content:
you can put them together with parentheses "( )"  and comma  ","

( $self.Z_D4 = 600,
  $self.Z_D4_Z = 5  )

if

$self.Z_MATERIAL_PRICE_GROUP = '88'
AND
$self.Z_PRODUCT_SIZE = 80

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Session | APAC | English
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with #SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to use the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Register Now

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: MBLB Add New Fields
entry.content:
Hi @Byz1 ,

First of all you need to add the required field in the internal table which is used to show the ALV, you can do this by implementing the below enhancement.

after adding the required field in Internal table you need to add this to field catalog, which you can do by implementing below enhancement.

Finally you can add the data to newly added field(s) using the same field catalog enhancement by looping itab internal table which is being used to display ALV, check below screenshot.

Please let me know if required any details.

Thanks and Best Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Excited to be a part of this community and look forward to both learning from and contributing to it.

entry.title: Field Value is not visible in SAP CAP List Report using Action
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP CAP NodeJs project List Report, in Action, I want to display the field value from my associated table. But I am not getting the field value inside the popup. Below are more details - 

Field 'EWMWarehouse' is from source table and its visible in the output; but field 'Batch' is from target (associated) table and it's value is not coming the output popup window as shown below - 

When I did inspect then I found the batch value is their in the response, but its not appearing in the above popup window.

Please help.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Bilingual Offer Letter
entry.content: this link is not working. cloud you please provide the correct link.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors RCM Issue while Scheduling Interview
entry.content:
Hi Sanjeet,

Thanks for your prompt response.

I would like to clarify that this is not a case of email duplication. In the audit trail of the candidate’s application within the talent pipeline, two separate email templates were triggered. One of these aligns correctly with the selected invitation template, while the other is unrelated and appears to have been triggered unexpectedly.

Regards,

Hamza

entry.title: Re: New Problem in for change Previous Period in MMPI T C...
entry.content:
Hi Vijay,

If material ledger is active, you cant open the closed MM Period using MMPI. 

Check the below note for details.

2042164 - MMPV Period shifted by error to future period for Material Ledger active plant

 

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Bill of Material API for outbound services
entry.content:
Hello Talke,

I am not aware of an outbound service. But you can use this API with events.

BOM API:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7489fa08cede494cbdf08fa3651598af/8a8b08aab6cd47f4a3e35e8bd8d86ca2.html?locale=en-US

Events for BOM: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7489fa08cede494cbdf08fa3651598af/e7f078a006e44e929c2f819117e23194.html?locale=en-US

General link to Public Cloud business events:
https://api.sap.com/package/SAPS4HANACloudBusinessEvents/overview

Regards Johannes
Johannes Schulte | Senior SAP Expert
Email: johannes.schulte@vico-finconsulting.vicoland.com
On behalf of: Vicoland GmbH
Website: www.vicoland.com/sap

entry.title: Re: Changing custom table delivery class from A to C
entry.content:
Changing the delivery class won't be a problem.

Just to be clear, though: you could also transport entries in a "A" table - just create a transport, and enter the keys of the relevant entries. The difference between "A" and "C" comes to bear when you maintain entries via a generated maintenance view, over SM30: here, for a class "C" table, you will be prompted for a transport - if the client is set (in SCC4) to "Automatic recording of changes".

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori launchpad - save as tile functionality
entry.content: @Jocelyn_DartPlease refer to the screenshot bellow.

entry.title: Re: Integration Advisor : Use of cXML Type System Exporting to CPI
entry.content:
@Sonali_Dash @ajmaradiaga 

 Could we please use this for punch out catalogue wherein from BTP IS we can connect to vendor portal site? Add carts and let those items back to S4.

S4 <->BTP IS <->Vendor portal

I see only c-xml capability but on OCI 😞

Please reply

entry.title: DML Perform in Crystal Report
entry.content: Hi, I want to perform DML in Crystal Reports. is it possible? and how?

entry.title: Re: Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@Sesh_Sreenivas 

On click on the "Deploy All" button the entire project is released as a version and that version is deployed. If there are other artefacts (like data type, automation, process) in the same project they are also included in the version deployment.

With the above statement, when we transport the artefacts from Dev, Qa, Prd environments, how does it work when the Business directly edits the Rules in the respective environments?
 - Does the new imported versions automatically adopt the OLD values in the respective environments? Or will it get overwritten? Or import needs to be done again?

entry.title: Re: Internal error in FORM/FUNCTION CKMLKEPH_ROLLUP in position 2 with RC 4
entry.content: I still don't understand why this error occurs when the settlement T-code Co-88 is run. Can you provide more details or any videos?dio

entry.title: Re: Joule error in S4HC public when accessing SAC: No acc...
entry.content:
Hello @nageshcaparthy

... any comment regarding my answer?

What is the plan for SAC customers using SAML/SSO with e.g. User ID?

This has to be changed for being able to use Joule?

For what reasons are these things created then?

- One managed OAUTH Client: JOULE_OAUTH_CLIENT_etc_etc...

- One Trusted Identity Provider: JOULE_IDP_etc_etc...

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Error when installing SAP B1 ServerTools
entry.content: Can you try to run setup.exe "run as administrator" ?

entry.title: Re: Information on reset and reverse document.
entry.content:
Hello,

Please check if the change log will give you the required information.

Also refer to this OSS note: https://me.sap.com/notes/0002785376 (FBRA will be visible in the change log).

Best Regards,
Henk Hoogerbrug

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow - London
entry.content:
Join the SAP ALM Roadshow and learn to shape your ALM Strategy, manage your on-premise and cloud, hybrid landscapes and get the most value from your SAP solutions.

Experience the empowerment through an in-person event. Network with experts to accelerate your implementations and efficiently operate your SAP landscape using SAP’s ALM portfolio. A great opportunity to engage with SAP's ALM experts and product management team.

Please click here for registration!

entry.title: Re: How to activate Business Catalog SAP_CMD_BC_SUPP_MAINT_PC to able the Migration Object Supplier
entry.content: Hi Davi,

entry.title: Re: Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation
entry.content: Nice! Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: How to activate Business Catalog SAP_CMD_BC_SUPP_MAINT_PC to able the Migration Object Supplier
entry.content: Have you checked the business Catalogs in the Target System (ie S/4HANA System) SAP_CMD_BC_SUPP_MAINT_PC (Master Data - Supplier Master)?

entry.title: Re: How many entries can be configured in custom attriubu...
entry.content: If you are dealing with so many entries, I would suggest to use an Attribute of type Dictionary Link and link it to a dedicated Dictionary Category. Dictionaries can be populated Manually, by Excel Import, Out of The Box Integrations (eg. LeanIX) or custom Integrations using the Signavio REST API.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC)
entry.content:
@Divya29  regarding issue below, it turned out to be an authorization issue. you may run SU53 to get the report and ask the support team to grant access.

---

Cannot open Simulate Configuration Models app

OTR-Based Exception of Class: CX_PMEVC_SIMULATION_ADAPTER

entry.title: Re: BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits
entry.content:
Hi @javierrubio_01 , please open an issue on git (https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-resource-consumption-monitor) so we can discuss it there. It would be good to provide more information about the error you receive (full url) and whether you can access the story directly in SAC.

Hi @vara_prasad_ , please open an issue on git (https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-resource-consumption-monitor) so we can discuss it there.It would be good to provide more information about the error you receive (open the Network tab to identify the error) and have a look at the 'known issues' about the cachebuster.

entry.title: Developing Mobile Apps with SAP Build
entry.content:
A Comprehensive Blog Series on Mobile Application Development with SAP’s latest development tool set

Over the course of last year, I’ve published several blog posts covering SAP Build Apps and connectivity with other SAP systems. In those posts I’ve used various products available in the SAP Build portfolio, such as SAP Build Code to develop a CAP-based data service and SAP Build Work Zone to publish a web application created in SAP Build Apps.

This year, we have combined the SAP Build portfolio into a new commercial offering with a simplified licensing model to ease adoption. SAP Build is the unified SAP low-code, pro-code and AI assisted development platform designed to modernize enterprise software and embrace digital transformation.

In this new blog series, I will build upon the data service created last year and use various options related to SAP Mobile Services to create (mobile) applications. The blog posts are set up like a tutorial and will provide actionable insights and detailed steps to guide developers effectively.

Step-by-Step Guide to Mobile Application Development with MDK

The following sections offer a detailed walkthrough of the mobile app development process using SAP Build and Mobile Development Kit (MDK). Each step is designed to ensure precision and clarity, enabling developers to replicate the process seamlessly.

Many tutorials and demos make use of unauthenticated data sources to avoid the rather tedious process of making the connections work. This makes sense for quick demos, but does not reflect the reality we are in. In this series, I am going to show you what it is really like to use authentication and role-based access, and how to work around some quirks that are currently in the toolchain.

This year, I've switched to Drawio for creating the diagram.

Prerequisite: CAP-based Data Service

As mentioned, I am going to continue the blog series based on what I’ve published last year and reuse the data service. A quick recap:

In Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - Connectivity options for low-code development - part 1 I have created a very simple data model in SAP Business Application Studio containing a Product entity, added some sample data, and made sure only authenticated users were able to access it. In the following blog post Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - Connectivity options for low-code development - part 2 I have added more fine grained Role-based access control.

A few posts later, Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - refining data sources with CAP while keeping a clean core showcases using CAP to combine the Product entity with a subset of external data from an S/4HANA system: Supplier information from BusinessPartnerA2X.

And in the last relevant part, Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - adding application logic with CAP while keeping the core clean I have added custom application logic in the data service to manipulate some of the data coming from the S/4HANA system.

Creating a Mobile Project in SAP Build Lobby

The SAP Build Lobby serves as the central hub for project creation and management. We begin by selecting the option to Create a new application project.

Notice that the SAP Build Lobby’s user interface has been revamped.

After selecting the Objective – build an Application, click Next and select Mobile. We could also use the Full-Stack option, which we’ve also used to create, test and deploy our data service in the previous blog series. However, for this blog post I will use the dedicated Mobile development environment. This environment contains more tools related to mobile use cases.

After selecting Next, we have basically one choice: Mobile Application. This enables us to develop platform-agnostic, metadata-based applications that can be created once and deployed across multiple platforms.

After selecting Next, we can give the project a name and description and have the choice to select a Dev Space. You can create a new one or select an existing one – if available. Take note that you cannot have more than two Dev Spaces running simultaneously with SAP Business Application Studio. If you already have two Dev Spaces running, then please navigate to the Dev Space Manager via the link Additional Application Types and shut down at least one of them before creating a new mobile specific Dev Space.

Select Review to move to the next step in the project creation wizard.

The summary page gives you a clear overview of your selected choices for you to review. If all looks good, please go ahead and select Create.

You will navigate back to the SAP Build Lobby main page where you can see your project being created. This can take a few seconds to up to a minute, as the Dev Space is being created and spun up.

 

Accessing the Mobile Project in SAP Business Application Studio

Once the project is created, select the project name, which is a link to open SAP Business Application Studio with your mobile Dev Space.

A mobile development perspective is opened within SAP Business Application Studio, providing access to tools specifically tailored for mobile application development. This environment facilitates integration, editing, debugging, and deployment activities for your mobile application.

 

Creating a new mobile app configuration in SAP Mobile Services

In the Storyboard view of SAP Business Application Studio, initiate the process of creating a new Mobile Services application by selecting the + button in the Runtime Resources section.

If you have not signed into the Cloud Foundry runtime of your target environment yet, then you will be asked to sign in now. The easiest way to do this, is by using the SSO Passcode and opening a new browser page to generate the passcode. Copy the passcode and paste it into the field to sign in. All can be done with just a few clicks.

Make sure to use the Cloud Foundry Endpoint from your target runtime environment, as it may differ from your development environment. You can find the endpoint in the Overview tab of the target subaccount’s BTP cockpit.

After signing in, you’ll need to select the Cloud Foundry Organization and Cloud Foundry Space.

Once signed in and the target is selected, the SAP Mobile Services instance active in this environment will be used to create a new application configuration. If there already application configurations defined in this SAP Mobile Services instance, then they will be listed and you could select one (but we won’t …).

Select the + button to create a new mobile application configuration. We will provide an Application Name and Application ID and select IAS for the Security Settings. We can skip the sample OData ESPM, as we have created our own data service.

In the Next step, we will configure a Mobile Services specific destination that is associated with the mobile application configuration. Note that this is not a BTP destination that will be visible in the BTP Cockpit. You will rather find it in the Mobile Services Admin UI.

First, we provide a Destination Name.

Most of the remaining items can be copied from the BTP Destination that was already created during the deployment of the CAP service, except for the client secret.

In the screenshot below I have opened the destination deployed together with the data service. Items to be copied:

  • Destination Type: select Internet Destination.
  • URL: the URL at which we have exposed our CAP-based data service.
  • SSO Mechanism: select OAuth2 User Token Exchange. This enables secure transmission of user identity (the "subject") between systems. This is especially useful in scenarios where a backend service (the "Sender") needs to act on behalf of a user when calling another service (the "Receiver").
  • Token Service URL: this is the endpoint where a client application sends a request to exchange credentials (like a client ID and secret) for an access token. This token is then used to authenticate subsequent API calls.
  • Token Service URL type: select Dedicated.
  • Client ID: This is a unique identifier assigned to an OAuth client (typically an application or service) that is registered with the Identity Authentication Service (IAS).

We have one item remaining that cannot be copied from the BTP destination: the client secret. It is hidden. Where can we find this secret? I’ll provide you one way to find it. I am sure there are more ways and maybe even easier way to get it (feedback is welcome 😉 ).

Navigate to the subaccount where you have deployed the CAP-based data service and open the Cloud Foundry Space where the service is running. Check the Instances tab and select the uaa instance (named: <your service name>-uaa) deployed with your data service and select the link View Credentials. You will find the “clientsecret” in the JSON structure shown.

Once the form is completed, click the Create App button. The application configuration will now be created in SAP Mobile Services. This will take a few minutes. Once configured successfully, the state will change to Started.

However, before we continue adding the app to the project, we must fix and enhance the configuration. For this we need to navigate to the SAP Mobile Services Admin UI. You can easily open the correct instance by selecting the Mobile Services link.  

Setting Up Cross Context Single Sign-On (SSO)

Navigate to the SAP Mobile Services Admin Cockpit to configure Cross Context Single Sign-On (SSO) for the newly defined mobile app. This feature in SAP Mobile Services allows users to reuse their OAuth tokens obtained in one context (like a desktop browser or mobile browser) in another context (like a native mobile app). This eliminates the need for users to log in again when switching between platforms.

In the application configuration, navigate to the Security settings.

Select the Edit button and tick Enable Cross Context SSO. Cross Context SSO streamlines user experiences by eliminating the need for multiple logins.

Click OK to update the Security settings and navigate back to the application’s Settings.

Fixing the Mobile Services Destination configuration

Now we need to resolve an issue with the destination settings. Navigate to the Connectivity settings.

As you can see, the destination that was created is not configured for Authentication. Select the destination and select Edit.

Ensure to configure the destination as described earlier. Luckily, most of the fields are already filled with the correct information. For the SSO Mechanism select OAuth2 User Token Exchange instead of No Authentication. Select Token Service URL Type : Dedicated. Click Next and Finish to save the destination configuration.

 

Validating the Destination Configuration

We can test the Destination within the SAP Mobile Services Admin Cockpit to verify its functionality and resolve potential issues before we even deploy the application to a mobile device. This ensures that the mobile application interacts seamlessly with backend systems, and we can avoid lengthy and troublesome debugging session on the mobile device.

When selecting Ping, the connection between SAP Mobile Services and the data source is checked. If this is not successful, then you need to check the URL you’ve specified.

Next to Ping, there is another button to open the OData Application Destination Test. Select this to open a simple UI that you can use to check the OData service.

You should be able to see entity data from Products, as well as entity data from MySupplier. If you do not see this data, then you’ll need to double check your configuration settings. There is no point adding the application to the project without access to this (meta)data, as the metadata is used to create the application’s data model and user interface structure.

Adding the App to the Project

Navigate back to SAP Business Application Studio, ensure the destination is selected and click the Add App to Project button.

Generating and Configuring a Passcode

This will initiate a passcode generation process. The passcode must be manually entered to validate access.

A notification in the bottom-right appears that helps you to open a hyperlink to create an SSO passcode.

Copy the passcode and go back to SAP Business Application Studio to paste the passcode into the prompted field at the top of the screen.

Checking the external resource’s data model

Once the application is added to the project you will see the Storyboard again, which is now updated with the newly added application and destination.

You can check the data model of the external resource by selecting the item and clicking View Diagram. This will open a graphical view of the mobile destination’s metadata, which is stored in an XML file. The model should be the same as what I’ve shown in the prerequisites section.

 

Creating the User Interface in Storyboard

Switch back to the Storyboard view and create a UI application. This will open a UI creation wizard to generate the MDK UI. You can change the application name if needed and select the MDK template type. In our case, we will go for the CRUD type, allowing users to create, read, update and delete entity data.

For the target MDK Client version, ensure the highest version is selected and enable auto-deployment to SAP Mobile Services after creation.

After selecting Next, on the second page of the wizard select Service Type OData. We do not want to enable mobile Offline capabilities because the data service we have created is not able to handle this.

We do want to select all data collections from the data source.

Select Finish. You will see an Action page prompting to overwrite the vscode settings. I think merging is the best option here (although you don't have that option).

After selecting Finish, the UI application is created and the application’s metadata bundle is deployed to SAP Mobile Services. Upon successful deployment, the MDK Application Editor is opened, showing an onboarding dialog containing a QR code.

Any SAP Mobile Services Client app can download the metadata from SAP Mobile Services after finishing the onboarding steps.

Loading and Previewing the Application

Now that the application has been deployed, we can use a so-called ‘player’ to ‘execute’ the combination of metadata and JavaScript on a device. It is possible to build such a player app yourself, customized to your needs. However, in most cases a generic client is good enough. We have published such a generic MDK client in the public app stores.

You can find the “SAP Mobile Services Client” in the Apple AppStore as well as the Google Play Store. Download and install this app on your device.

You can now scan the QR code with the Mobile Svcs app.

A short video of the mobile onboarding process can be seen below. Steps:

  1. Open SAP Mobile Services Client app
  2. Scan QR code
  3. Confirm onboarding URL
  4. Sign in to custom IdP
  5. Set up passcode
  6. Enable Face ID (for Apple devices)
  7. Client connects to SAP Mobile Services instance and checks for application metadata availability
  8. Confirm downloading app metadata update
  9. Run app

 

Conclusion

In this blog post I have outlined the steps to create a cross-platform mobile application in SAP Build starting from the SAP Build Lobby, all the way to having the application running on a mobile device. The mobile user of the app can access business data that is protected through authorization and role-based access control. 

This serves as a starting point to further application development steps.

As always, feedback is welcome to improve the blog posts, but most importantly the products involved.

entry.title: Re: Fiori: Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 ()
entry.content: hi Tracy , can you please help on how to apply this sap Note ?

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for Managing Master Data Across Scenar...
entry.content: Hi @Sotiris_X, for PaPM on-premise/ PCE you may consider to use RFA of HANA external procedure type. You may refer to Thomas' blog for more details: https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blog-posts-by-sap/papm-how-to-embed-an-sap-hana-stored-procedure-into-a-model/ba-p/13388729

entry.title: Re: cXML Support in Trading Partner Management of SAP Int...
entry.content:
@Sonali_Dash @ajmaradiaga

S4 <->BTP IS <->Vendor portal

entry.title: Re: How to create and use your own ATC check variant in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi @Ram_peddireddi,

the error happens probably because in the check "Usage of Released APIs" the parameter ABAP Language Version has the value 0, which must be 1 for ABAP development (or 2 if using key user tools):

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA PP module stock requirement for Sales pendin...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can export data in excel via using tocde MD04P available in S4/HANA.

Stock/Requirement Pegging (MD04P) and MRP Statuses 

Regards

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: Modeling Update (Join)
entry.content:
For all SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM) modelers, get ready to experience environment modeling like never before! We're incredibly excited to unveil our latest upgrade, packed with innovative features designed to provide better user experience and satisfaction!

In this blogpost allow me to guide you through the latest upgrade in Modeling screen starting with the Join function. The goal is not only to provide a more user-friendly interface but also to enable a faster navigation through function configuration.

Ready to supercharge your modeling experience? Let’s get started!

As the title suggests, this Modeling screen upgrade will start in Join function. As you already know, in our Join function we have different nodes such as: Input, Signature, Join Rules, and Checks. Now, the nodes such as Input, Signature, and Checks will be in Properties panel, leaving only the Join Rules node in the Join function itself.

Let’s go through the highlight of the new functionalities that a modeler can maximize in these upgrades.

Just like before, Input Function and Input (for field) still works with dropdown functionality and the Complex Selections option in Input still works with Edit Calculation button if a complex selection needs to be maintained. 

The cool added functionality now is, when a specific field needs to have a Formula or a Value Selection, you can use the Edit (Edit Selection & Mapping) button which will give you the ability to maintain the needed formula and selection just like in the old days 😉

You might also have noticed already these two buttons next to the Input?

Yes! This is one of the features that we added in this enhancement. These are the new Copy and Paste buttons which will allow the users to copy a created field inside the modeling and paste it into this specific section. How cool is that? These Copy and Paste buttons are also located and can be maximized in Signature and Checks.

And do not worry, the dropdown functionality is still in place if the user wants to manually choose fields to be maintained in either Granularity, Selection, or Actions Fields. How cool is that!

Finally, in Checks, not only is it now easier to copy and paste the checks in the Properties panel, but modelers can also maximize the use of the new Checks dropdown option!

Checks are now grouped, so modelers will have an easy way to identify which check to select before running the function instead of expanding the checks node. Now that’s convenient!

Except for these user interface updates to the Input, Signature, and Checks nodes, everything else will stay the same, including the configuration of the Join Rules,  Since all our previous setups will continue to function, modelers don't need to take any additional action.

I hope this blogpost provides you with helpful information about this first modeling screen upgrade. Soon, this upgrade will also be applied to all functions so that modelers will have a faster and easier modeling experience! To know which function is next, you can visit our What’s New series where we announce new features and upgrades for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud – Standard Model! Thank you for reading!

entry.title: Re: BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits
entry.content: Hi @Ganesha you can ignore this warning, the import should work fine.

entry.title: Re: Sample UI5 CRUD Operations
entry.content: can anybody help me with step by step guide with commands so i can also achieve the screenshot like result

entry.title: Re: Custom calculation of coverage (similar to Days of Su...
entry.content: Of course you can try to do any kind of disaggregation. Inside the optimizer we do it a little bit simpler: If we need a coverage of 1.5 weeks wee the into account all demands of the first week and 50% of all demands of the second week. We avoid the disaggregation onto days, but plan on the level of weeks.

entry.title: How to auto populate custom fields in coding block of clearing document posted using f-03
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to auto populate the custom fields in coding block of clearing document line items from the original document line items. Is there any config setting available to configure the flow of data from original document to clearing document? Or is there any enhancement option available to achieve this?

Thanks in advance

Poornima

entry.title: H1_2025 P4 | Manage Pending Hire RBP permission issue
entry.content:
is there other customers impacted by these change in Production regarding RBP permission

Manage Pending Hire

These RBP permission is not taken into consideration so HR people having these Role don't see the scope of People they are expected to see. Count for Hirable Candidate for Recruiting Remains to ZERO instead of showing all the candidates that are matching these condition.

entry.title: Re: Error C 056 “Field overflow during price calculation”...
entry.content:
Hi @gvanessa18 
For this kind of Issue, It is always better to provide PO details to verify the Issue
As we don't know the exact quantity and value of the PO - GR scenario..
(like multiple PO items GR or PO Item has more quantity or PO Item has more value etc)
One suggestion would be - we may need to maintain Order Price Unit (OPU) in the PO Item then Maintain corresponding conversion factor in PO Item > Quantities/Weights tab. Please Verify the value details in Item conditions tab and proceed for GR posting.
(Conversion between Order Unit - OUn and  OPU)
It is always recommended to do this activity in your test system for better clarification.

entry.title: SAP Business ByDesign Archive Option Setup
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are preparing the Archive Option and would like to confirm the following points:

1. Tenant Usage
- Should we use our existing production tenant ?(e.g. myXXXXXX.sapbydesign.com)
Is this correct for Archive Option?

Service Control Center ▶ Licenses ▶ Users:
Licensed/Assinged Users = 0
 Under these conditions, can we continue to use the same tenant as our Archive environment?

2. Archive-View-Only Role
- Can we create a custom “Archive Viewer” role with view-only permissions?
- If so, which specific permissions/views must be included?

3. License Count Verification
- After assigning the Archive Viewer role to users, can we confirm successful configuration by checking in:
Service Control Center ▶ Licenses ▶ Users that:
• Licensed Users = 0
• Assigned Users = 0


Any missing information or additional steps we should follow?
Thank you for your help!

Best regards,

Masahiro

entry.title: Re: Maintain PIRs doesn't save PIRs
entry.content: @egil63 The note mentions dumps during PIR saving. Check and analyze dumps in ST22. If you can't resolve the issue based on this analysis, report the problem to SAP support.

entry.title: How to Redirect SAP CC Backoffice to Custom Domain
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We’ve recently set up a new domain specifically for accessing the SAP Convergent Charging (SAP CC) Backoffice. The application is currently accessible via:

https://myDomain.com/backoffice/login.zul

We would like to simplify this so that users can access the Backoffice directly via:

https://myDomain.com

Is there a recommended way to configure a redirect or rewrite so that accessing the root domain https://myDomain.com automatically takes users to /backoffice/login.zul?

Any best practices, configuration examples, or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,

Mohsen Ahyaie

Do we have something similar available in S/4 HANA Public Cloud?

Has anyone faced this issue before, and is there a configuration or workaround available to control how negative signs appear in the Excel export?

Thank you in advance for your support!

# @HCM @EC-Payroll

we maintain KFs at daily level, so I have stock calculated in days which has to be considered in days in lets say 3 weeks and then in weeks  - after. I am thinking how to setup it correctly that being an input for Optimizer it considers it properly at both levels

entry.title: Re: Manual rebate process: Tax statement item missing for...
entry.content:
Hello,

When you encounter the ‘Tax statement item missing for tax Code XA’ error during manual settlement using WB2R_DOC_Entry, it usually implies that the tax line items weren’t correctly processed in the final settlement. Here are some key points and potential resolutions:

Causes

  1. Timing of Delta Accruals and Final Settlement: The issue arises when delta accrual and final settlement dates coincide on the same day, possibly leading to conflicts between these processes and missing tax line items.

  2. Tax Classification and Setup: Although you’ve correctly set up tax classifications for both customers and materials, there might be an oversight in processing tax during settlement.

Potential Solutions

  1. Re-check Tax Setup: Verify if all mandatory tax parameters (like tax amount, tax base amount) are correctly passed in the settlement process. Ensure that all settings for accrual and final settlement are accurately configured without conflicts.

  2. Separate Accrual and Settlement Dates: Consider separating the dates for delta accrual and final settlement to prevent processing overlap. This might help in correctly generating tax statement items for final settlement.

  3. Manual Error Analysis: Using error logs and material price analysis tools, analyze the costing run errors for adjustments. Refer to SAP’s guidance on handling incorrect settlement manually. Inventory Accounting - Processing Incorrect Settlement Manually

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: The New Rendering Experience – Active Viewport Rendering
entry.content:
Hello and good morning:

Thanks, great post 👍


What I have observed at a customer though is, that ACTIVE VIEWPORT RENDERING seems to have some overhead, which becomes "visible" if the SAC story with all its widgets fits on the screen of the end user's device right away. By switching ACTIVE VIEWPORT RENDERING OFF we increased the performance, cutting the run time approx. in half.

Of course your mileage might vary-.

Thanks,
Andreas J.A.

entry.title: Re: RAP event trigger via SAP GUI tcode
entry.content:
Hi @jitendra_it ,

Sales Order RAP Events will also be triggered from SAP GUI. I just tested and double checked it.

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: MBLB Add New Fields
entry.content: Basically check this answer MBLB New Fields (provided and accepted in your first post)

entry.title: DRC eDocument .html file / Tax Payer ID
entry.content:
Hey Community,

we are currently implementing the DRC tool incl. eDocument for Hungary. We need to change the Tax Payer ID field in the .html file. Unfortunately we cannot find the system setting where it can be changed.

 

On company code level I can enter a tax number, but it is passed in the field “Community Vat Number”.

Does anyone have an idea?

Regards Christian

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance 
#eDocument

entry.title: Re: 1GP different tax countries are not permitted
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

You're seeing the error “1GP different tax countries are not permitted. V0 tax code was used” because even the V0 tax code (which means no tax) still triggers tax logic in SAP.

So, when you post between two company codes in different tax countries, SAP checks for tax consistency even if no tax is calculated.

 

Why is this happening ? :

  • V0 is still a tax code → SAP checks tax countries.
  • If the companies are in different countries, SAP blocks it—even if it’s zero tax.

 

How to fix it ?:

  • Best option: Just remove the tax code (leave it blank) if there’s truly no tax.
  • Or, only use V0 if both companies are in the same tax country.

 

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: About 1-5 times per week our HA SQLA 17 database will begin pausing
entry.content:
I don't know such a command (but also have never used HA in production).

What does system procedure sa_mirror_server_status() show?

Have you tried whether log offset-based database properties like CurrentReDoPos or LastCommitRedoPos and the like, requested from both partner servers, reveal helpful information (though these would apparently return log offsets, not anything "time-related")? (I'd think that the mentioned system procedure does internally request those, too, but it has no published definition.)


Aside: It's so dissatisfying that this huge forum only offers to add a comment via a single-line text box control, so I basically always have to edit it immediately to apply any kind of formatting or the like.

entry.title: How to get notified by email when Application Autoscaler scales down to 1 instance?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m using Application Autoscaler on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry to reduce the number of instances of my application when the load drops. This works as expected — I can see that the instance count scales down to 1 based on the defined policy.

What I want to achieve is:
Receive an email notification automatically whenever the application scales down, especially when it reaches 1 instance.

My Questions:

  • Is it possible to configure this directly within the policy.json file used by the autoscaler?

  • If not, is there any built-in mechanism in BTP (without writing custom code or external integrations) that can notify us when such a scaling event happens?

  • Is there a way to connect autoscaler events to an email notification flow, fully within BTP services?

Thanks in advance for any suggestions or examples.

entry.title: Create custom entity with compositions exposed as OData V4 API
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I have a question which I am not able to solve by myself ^^

My requirement basically is to create a new custom API in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud that allows me, for a given {warehouse + batch-managed product + quantity}, to find the list of all batch numbers, that can be consumed to address that batch-managed product's quantity based on some rules.

I gave myself as objective to create this structure as output of my new custom API, with a header structure (giving the status of the batch determination logic) and an item list (giving the list of all the batch numbers that should be used for the consumption, with the quantity to be consumed in each batch):

I started by created 2 custom CDS entities: one for the header structure and another one for the item structure.

I added this annotation to both these 2 custom CDS entities "@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy:'ABAP:ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1'" where "ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1'" is a custom class referencing interface "IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER" In order to be able to trigger its method "SELECT" when someone tries to retrieve data from either one of my 2 custom CDS entities. 

In order to have a parent-child relationship between my 2 custom CDS entities, I added a composition statement in the header custom entity CDS view and an association to parent statement in the line-items child custom entity CDS view.

Custom Entity for the Header parent CDS view:

Custom Entity for the Line-Item Child CDS view:

Then I implemented code for the SELECT statement of my custom class ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1.

Once done, I created a new service definition exposing both my 2 custom cds entities:

Then I exposed my service definition into an OData V4 API service binding, followed with the normal communication scenario, comm arrangement configuration..

My problem is that, once all this is done, when I try to execute my API from POSTMAN, I am not able to get the header AND the item data AT THE SAME TIME.

I am able for instance to get data for the header custom entity CDS view with the correct structure I defined: 

I am also able to get data for the line_item custom entity CDS view, for the different batch numbers to use for the consumption as per the structure I designed:

But, How can I merge these two results of these 2 entities in the same response?

I want my custom API response to be similar to the following one: 

How can I accomplish this please?

I thought by creating a composition relationship between these 2 custom entities, that the SELECT method will fetch data for both header and Item custom entity CDS views and that the result will be merged in the same response, but it is not.

Also, when I run a GET OData call to the header entity, "SELECT" method of class "ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET0" is called only once, and "io_request->get_entity_id( )" gives "ZC_TEST_BATCH_DETERMIN_HD" as entity. This means, that my logic is being executed ONLY on the header custom CDS view, which is not enough to fulfill my requirement.

Then I tried to add "$expand" to the OData URL to execute, by adding "?$expand=_BatchDetItemDetails" to my URL:

but I am getting HTTP Bad Request error in POSTMAN : "

"The expand option's expand path contains an invalid property '_BatchDetItemDetails'",

Can someone help me on this please? I feel like my custom API is not designed to "accept" any "expand" attribute. 

Or if you have another idea on how to achieve this, without having to use the "?$expand" statement?

Thanks experts

entry.title: Re: Getting ICMENOSESSION error while calling SAP S4 webservice from SAP CPI
entry.content:
Is there a compelling reason, why you can't simply use RFC directly? If the interface is originally available as RFC-enabled function module, then this is usually the better choice. Switching to a different protocol, just because it is "more modern", is a bad idea, in my opinion.

(BTW: interesting, that people are trying to "modernize" RFC interfaces by replacing them with HTTP-based interfaces... The HTTP protocol is older than the RFC protocol...! 🙂 HTTP was designed for transferring text files (HTML pages) via "one-shot" connections. RFC was designed for transferring business data (mix of text, floating point, BCD, integer data) via stateful connections. Consequently, RFC is better suited when you try to transfer business data: it is easier to use, more robust and much faster. Performance tests have shown:

  • in fast networks (LAN) - where serialization/deserialization time is the main contributor - RFC is about 14-19 times faster than WebService/SOAP
  • in slow networks (internet) - where network time is the main contributor - RFC is 4-5 times faster than WebService/SOAP

depending on the scenario and the type of data.)

entry.title: Re: PIR not consumed by goods issue in Strategy group 11
entry.content: Thank you for your feedback. With the combination of Strategy 11 and consumption mode 2 with Mixed MRP type 2, we are able to fulfill it.

entry.title: Re: What is planned for the 2505 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing?
entry.content:
Another nice small new feature that we forgot to mention: You can now control for a local extension implementation if the call should be buffered: Buffering Extension Calls of Local Extensions | SAP Help Portal

Same was already possible for remote extensions by returning response header Cache-Control: no-store: Buffering Extension Calls of Remote Extensions | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: PIR not consumed by goods issue in Strategy group 11
entry.content: I am quite new to PP world, We can communicate more if you like.

entry.title: Discrete, Process, or Repetitive? Understanding the Manufacturing Options (For Beginners!) 🏭
entry.content:
SAP Production Planning (PP): Discrete, Process, or Repetitive? Understanding Your Manufacturing Options

Welcome to the world of SAP Production Planning (PP)! If you're new to SAP, or just looking to understand how businesses manage their manufacturing, one of the first things you'll encounter is the concept of different production types. SAP PP offers flexible approaches to cater to diverse manufacturing scenarios.

Choosing the right type in SAP is crucial because it dictates how materials are managed, how production orders are structured, and how costs are tracked. Let's break down the three primary manufacturing options in SAP PP for beginners.

1. Discrete Manufacturing: Building Unique Products, Piece by Piece

Imagine a factory assembling cars, building complex electronics, or crafting custom furniture. These are perfect examples of Discrete Manufacturing.

What defines it?

  • Individual Products: You produce distinct, identifiable products, often with serial numbers or batch numbers. Each finished good is a unique item, even if it's part of a larger series.
  • Production Orders: Production is typically driven by specific production orders, where each order is created for a defined quantity of a particular product.
  • Flexible Production Lines: Production lines and processes can be reconfigured frequently to adapt to different product models or customer specifications.
  • Material Issuance: Materials are issued specifically to individual production orders, ensuring precise cost tracking for each manufactured item.
  • Bill of Materials (BOM) & Routing: These are heavily used to define what components go into a product and how it's manufactured (the sequence of operations).

When to use it: When your products are distinct, often complex, and require detailed tracking of each assembly step and component consumption.

2. Process Manufacturing: Mixing, Blending, and Transforming

Now, shift your focus to industries that create products by mixing, blending, or reacting ingredients. Think about chemicals, pharmaceuticals, food, beverages, or even paints. This is the realm of Process Manufacturing.

What defines it?

  • Batch-Oriented Production: Products are typically manufactured in batches. Ingredients are combined, processed, and transformed into a final product or an intermediate.
  • Recipes/Formulas (not BOMs): Instead of a Bill of Materials, Process Manufacturing uses "Recipes" or "Formulas" to define the quantities of ingredients and the sequence of processing steps.
  • Liquid, Powder, Gas Handling: This type often involves managing bulk materials like liquids, powders, or gases that are difficult to count as discrete units.
  • Resource Management Focus: There's a strong emphasis on managing resources like tanks, reactors, and pipelines, as well as tracking by-products or co-products that may result from the process.
  • Process Orders: Production is executed using "Process Orders" which are tailored to the specific batch or formula being produced.

When to use it: When your products are created through a series of transformations, mixing, or chemical reactions, and often involve batch-specific characteristics.

3. Repetitive Manufacturing (REM): High Volume, Consistent Flow

Imagine an automotive assembly line churning out thousands of identical car parts, or a factory producing vast quantities of standardized consumer goods like batteries or light bulbs. This is where Repetitive Manufacturing (REM) shines.

What defines it?

  • High Volume & Standardization: REM is designed for continuous, high-volume production of the same, standardized products over long periods.
  • Quantity and Period-Based: Instead of individual production orders, production is planned and executed based on quantities per period (e.g., "produce 500 units of X per day/week").
  • Production Lines (not orders): The focus is on the production line itself, and materials are typically issued to the line, not to specific, individual orders.
  • Streamlined Processes: It aims for simplicity and efficiency, reducing the need for detailed order-specific tracking once the line is set up.
  • Backflushing: Materials consumption and activity confirmations are often "backflushed" (automatically posted) at the end of the production cycle or when a certain quantity is completed, further streamlining the process.

When to use it: When you have a stable product design, a high and consistent demand, and production occurs on dedicated, repetitive lines.

Choosing the Right Path in SAP PP

Selecting the correct manufacturing type in SAP PP is a foundational decision. It directly impacts how your master data (materials, BOMs, routings, recipes), planning processes (MRP), and execution steps (production/process orders, confirmations) are configured and used.

Understanding these distinctions is your first step to mastering SAP Production Planning. Each type is optimized to handle different business realities, ensuring that SAP accurately reflects and supports your manufacturing operations.

entry.title: SAP Document Management ISO:27001/GoBD/DSGVO German Standards

-GoBD

-DSGVO 

kind regards, 

Philipp

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone Performance
entry.content:
Dear Community,

we are close to GoLive with SAP Work Zone SuccessFactors Edition and experience massive drops in the performance of the overall page loading times. 

The home page as currently a loading time of about 20 seconds, which is not acceptable for Work Zone to be used as the new HR Portal for all employees.

Are there any ideas, what we can do to improve the performance? Or is this a SAP topic? We also already created a ticket with support, however mayber there are some valuable insights from the community.

Thank you for any help,
Sebastian

SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone

entry.title: Re: Getting error in Eclipse ADT "Business Catalog is not...
entry.content: Thank you Sandra and Peter for your inputs. Appreciate it!

entry.title: 🚀 Bye-bye IDocs, Hello Modern SAP S/4HANA Integration! 🚀
entry.content:
Beyond IDocs: Navigating Modern Integration in SAP S/4HANA

For decades, if you talked about integrating systems with SAP, the term "IDoc" was almost certainly part of the conversation. These Intermediate Documents have been a foundational technology, reliably facilitating data exchange between SAP and non-SAP systems. However, as businesses push for real-time insights, cloud-native solutions, and more agile processes, the integration landscape within SAP S/4HANA is rapidly evolving.

It's time to look beyond the traditional and explore the powerful, modern alternatives that offer enhanced flexibility, blazing speed, and a better alignment with contemporary architectural demands. The shift isn't just about new technology; it's about enabling a truly connected enterprise.

Why Move Beyond Traditional IDocs?

While IDocs still have their place, especially in legacy scenarios, modern business requirements often call for more:

  • Faster and More Efficient Data Exchange: In today's real-time world, batch processing might not be enough. New integration methods enable instant data flow.
  • Better Alignment with Modern Integration Standards: APIs, for instance, are the lingua franca of modern application development, making it easier to connect diverse systems.
  • Enhanced Flexibility and Scalability: Cloud-native solutions offer the ability to scale integration capabilities up or down as needed, without massive upfront infrastructure investments.
  • Improved Real-time Capabilities: Critical for scenarios like e-commerce, real-time inventory updates, or immediate financial postings.

The Key Players in Modern SAP S/4HANA Integration

So, what are the alternatives you should be exploring in the SAP S/4HANA era?

  1. APIs (OData, REST, SOAP): The Real-time Connectors APIs are the backbone of modern, agile integration. They allow for standardized, performant, and often real-time communication between systems.

    • OData: A standardized protocol for building and consuming RESTful APIs, heavily used in SAP Fiori apps and often preferred for exposing SAP data.
    • REST (Representational State Transfer): A widely adopted architectural style for networked applications, offering lightweight and flexible communication.
    • SOAP (Simple Object Access Protocol): While considered more traditional than REST, SOAP is still robust and used for complex enterprise integrations requiring strong typing and strict contracts.

  2. SAP Ariba Cloud Integration Gateway (CIG): Streamlining Procurement For businesses leveraging the Ariba Network for procurement (think buying, invoicing, catalogs), CIG is a game-changer. It provides a seamless, standardized, and pre-packaged way to connect your SAP S/4HANA system to the Ariba Network. This eliminates much of the custom development typically required for buyer-supplier integration, leading to faster implementation and reduced operational costs.

  3. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Integration Suite: Your Cloud-Native Integration Hub The BTP Integration Suite is a comprehensive, cloud-native integration platform-as-a-service (iPaaS) offering. It's designed to handle a wide array of integration scenarios, from simple point-to-point connections to complex orchestration.

    • Capabilities: Includes capabilities for API management, enterprise messaging, open connectors, data intelligence, and process orchestration.
    • Flexibility: It's a powerhouse for integrating SAP with non-SAP systems, cloud-to-cloud, and on-premise-to-cloud scenarios.
    • Scalability: Being cloud-native, it offers inherent scalability and resilience, adapting to fluctuating business demands.

  4. SAP S/4HANA Output Management: Modern Document Handling This isn't just about data exchange between systems, but about how your SAP S/4HANA system communicates outward with documents like invoices, purchase orders, or shipping notifications. The new Output Management framework offers a flexible and modern way to handle these outputs, supporting various channels (print, email, EDI) and formats, moving away from older SAPscript or Smart Forms methods.

  5. File-Based Integration: Still Relevant for Specific Scenarios While the focus is on real-time APIs, file-based integration still holds value for certain use cases, especially with large data volumes or where real-time updates aren't critical. Modern file-based approaches often involve secure file transfer protocols and intelligent parsing mechanisms, sometimes orchestrated via BTP Integration Suite.

The Integration Evolution

The move away from IDoc-centric integration in SAP S/4HANA is a testament to the broader digital transformation happening across enterprises. These new integration technologies empower businesses to:

  • Respond to market changes more swiftly.
  • Automate end-to-end processes more effectively.
  • Gain real-time visibility across their operations.
  • Build more resilient and agile IT landscapes.

Understanding these modern integration paradigms is crucial for anyone working with or planning to implement SAP S/4HANA. It's not just about technical knowledge, but about envisioning a more connected and efficient future for your business.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors WorkZone Page Load Times
entry.content:
Hi @JohnRTx, 

are you still struggling with the performance or could you solve this in the meantime?
We are also facing the same issues.

Best Regards,
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: How to Prepare for the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification (with Sample Questions)
entry.content: This is a great guide you've out together, thanks for helping our community members pursue their certifications!

entry.title: Re: Inbound delivery released after packing not appearing...
entry.content: Since this is not Decentralized System both ERP and EWM are in same platform so IDN is not used here

entry.title: Auto convert just the English words for Amount total to Thai Language in Crystal Report Design
entry.content:
Hi All

How to Auto convert just the English words for Amount total to Thai Language in Crystal Report Design?

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: SAP SF EC WF Email Notifications
entry.content:
Hi, 

An initiator applied for a leave. That leave request should go to the approver as an email notification saying, 'You have received a workflow request.' Once the approver approves the workflow, a notification should be sent back to the initiator that the leave is approved. So, if we want to define who should receive which notification and when — how can we do that? Here, how does the workflow email notification go to the approver, and how does the email notification about the approved workflow request go to the initiator?

entry.title: Re: Ariba: Invoice issue
entry.content: if POs are created in SAP, these should come to Ariba with updated line item numbers as 10, 20, 30. This is what I used to see among other customers.

entry.title: Re: change PO item short text when transferring shipment cost
entry.content:
I am not sure if your scenario is the same/ is similar with posting freight settlement documents. If not it may still help you to know /scmtms/cl_post_sfir_for_accru METHOD /scmtms/if_post_sfir_for_accru~post_sfir_for_accurals.

Further on:

Please note the line highlighted, it points to the BADI /scmtms/badi_modify_po_data.
Also it's good to know that BAPI_PO_CREATE1 is called not BAPI_PO_CREATE which means some limitations, ie. custom fields.
Here is a code snippet of an impl. of that BADI:

  DATA: lo_post_sfir     TYPE REF TO /scmtms/cl_post_sfir_for_accru,
        lt_tor_item_data TYPE /scmtms/t_tor_item_tr_k.

* Get Instance for helper
  lo_post_sfir = /scmtms/cl_post_sfir_for_accru=>get_instance( ).
  DATA(lo_srv_mgr_tor)   = /bobf/cl_tra_serv_mgr_factory=>get_service_manager( iv_bo_key = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_bo_key ).

* Read Freight Order + Freight Settlement Information
  CALL METHOD lo_post_sfir->get_data
    IMPORTING
      et_sfir_item_data = DATA(lt_sfir_item_data) "Settlement Item Data
      et_tor_root_data  = DATA(lt_tor_root_data). "Freight Order Root Data

  lo_srv_mgr_tor->retrieve_by_association(
    EXPORTING
      iv_node_key    = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_node-root
      it_key         = CORRESPONDING /bobf/t_frw_key( lt_tor_root_data MAPPING key = root_key )
      iv_association = /scmtms/if_tor_c=>sc_association-root-item_tr
      iv_fill_data   = abap_true
    IMPORTING
      et_data        = lt_tor_item_data ).


" ...
" ...
 LOOP AT ct_po_items ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_po_items>).
      <ls_po_items>-plant = lv_plant.
    ENDLOOP.
    LOOP AT ct_po_itemsx ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_po_itemsx>).
      <ls_po_itemsx>-plant = abap_true.
    ENDLOOP.
" ...
" ...

There the PO item text can also be changed, I think.
It can also be done by enhancing development object /SCMTMS/CL_POST_SFIR_FOR_ACCRU: 

" ...
LOOP AT et_poitem ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_poitem>).
      <fs_poitem>-short_text = |{ <fs_poitem>-short_text } - { lv_group_id }|.
    ENDLOOP.
" ...


Notice also the post methods for SET_TMKOMK_COUNTRY_FIELDS and GET_INSTANCE.
If the country fields (or possibly other KOMK/KOMP fields derived from TM) are relevant to you, then it will not be enough to change SET_TMKOMK_COUNTRY_FIELDS due to a SAP bug in GET_INSTANCE. 

" GET_INSTANCE method with signature iv_ebeln, iv_ebelp
" iv_ebeln and iv_ebelp are never used 
" reproduce by: call me23n, open a PO created by TM, open another PO created by TM without leaving the transaction
" => same lo_sfir_srv_mgr instance will be used which is used during MM pricing

    lo_sfir_srv_mgr = /bobf/cl_tra_serv_mgr_factory=>get_service_manager( iv_bo_key = /scmtms/if_suppfreightinvreq_c=>sc_bo_key ).
    IF mo_stm_data IS NOT BOUND.

entry.title: Re: I have requirement of restricting the user to change...
entry.content:
AFAIK there are no implicit or explicit enhancement available between this input and (depending on activation of new general ledger and selection of one variant) execution of dialog maintenance on the following views V_T001B and V_T001BB or V_T001B_COFI and V_T001B_COFIB.

You could try to add an event in relevant maintenance dialogs Or create your own  transactions based on those views with a selection of variant?

entry.title: Certificate C_S4CPB_2502
entry.content:
Hi Team,

in the latest update for the certificate C_S4CPB_2502 (SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA) it is necessary to go through the steps in the following learning journey, before certification: Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Now I have an issue on step 4: Practical Test - Showcase your skills by applying your acquired skills practically

The link to the system loads and the restricted system is displayed, but I am not able to execute any action there. After I receive the required task, the system is not responsive: learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/hands-on/practical-test-873

So I am stuck.  Can you please check the system on your end and suggest me how to proceed? I guess that this issue will prevent any user to get certified. 

Thanks for your support

entry.title: Cayman (KY) calculation procedure
entry.content:
Hi All,

I created a new company code in the Cayman Islands,

and when I open an order for this company, an error occurs in FV60

"FF703 - Company code not assigned to country or country to calculation procedure"

There appears to be no SAP standard calculation procedure for the Cayman Islands. Should I build?

There are some articles in the community, but it seems that there is no conclusion

Thanks All !!

entry.title: Re: Script recording and Playback functionality
entry.content:
Check below

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/8e90085787614b65817996160020a2f3/c852df5370409438e10000000a44176d.html

entry.title: Re: Sending PR from Ariba to S/4HANA
entry.content:
usually one realm = one ERP. that is the whole point of FPC design where you have multiple child realms (each connected to one ERP)

If you have two ERPs as your backend obviously you need a middleware that will be your router.

So the middleware will receive the PO and send it to the right ERP.

Usually routing is done based on accounting entity, for example Company Code number.

entry.title: Re: Getting error in Eclipse ADT "Business Catalog is not...
entry.content: Peter and Sandra, thank you so much. I appreciate you both for providing inputs. Per your reply and based on responses in other blogs, I understood that we don't need Business catalog when we are in private cloud. And just FYI, if I copy the standard template in Application job template screen, I am able to get the custom template in the Application jobs screen. On top of it, if I add my custom Application template as step2, I can see under application jobs, but when i try to remove standard template, again it is not showing in Application jobs. Assuming it to be authorizations issue, I will connect with Fiori people and look into the new authorizations note 3190922.

entry.title: Re: Run Statutory Reports App custom billing document num...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

This is a known behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition particularly in the "Run Statutory Reports" Fiori app and other similar analytical/reporting apps. When exporting data to Excel, negative numbers often appear with a leading minus sign (e.g. : -100) instead of in parentheses or locale-specific formats (e.g. : (100)).

Explanation:

The format of numbers in Excel exports is not controlled by SAP GUI or Fiori UI formatting settings, but by:

  1. The default SAP export service format, which uses standard data representations.
  2. Excel’s own formatting, which treats all incoming data as raw text or numbers unless explicitly formatted.

Is there a standard configuration to change this?

Unfortunately, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition does not provide a direct configuration setting to change the display of negative numbers specifically in Excel exports from the “Run Statutory Reports” app.

 

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Remove the permission to Insert New Record via histor...
entry.content: Hello @Cambria_Vandiver, thank you for your response. This is very helpful.

entry.title: Wage Type Calculated based on another wage type
entry.content:
Dears,

I have a business case, a blue collar is working and getting an incentive based on the percentage of productivity, we need to record the maximum amount of the incentive as an info wage type and whenever we insert the percentage of achievement a calculation to be done, the recorded amount will be divided by 30 days then multiplied by the number of working days the multiplied by the achieved percentage

The main issue is this employee can work within the month on behalf of his manager due to absence, so the business wants to record 2 amounts with 2 different periods within the same month.

for example: the payroll period is from day 16 of the previous month till 15 in the current one. So, the employee is working as a supervisor from day 16 till day 9 with a maximum amount of incentive 3000 EGP and achievement percentage 70%, and form day 10 till day 15 with a maximum amount of incentive 1500 EGP and achievement percentage 40%, so the calculation should be (((3000/30*25) *70%)+((1500/30*6) *40%)).

I've created the incentive maximum amount in IT8 as to be able to change it with in the month and the PCR can read the number of days, while the recorded percentage has been created in IT15, but whenever i run the payroll the 2 records for the incentives come in the RT accumulated with total amount 4,500 and the percentage as well with an amount 110%, so i get the end result with a wrong amount. I've tried to change processing class 20 but with no output. 

So please does anyone have any suggestion how can i achieve this business case.

 

Kind Regards
